title: Frame Budgets, Profiling, Allocations, Collision Cost, and Responsiveness · Language Clinic: Representative Profile
summary: The team optimizes by feel, reports average FPS, profiles menus instead of peak play, allocates every draw, rebuilds masks per frame, and changes physics for speed. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the representative profile increment in representative frame-time and capacity evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Profile warm representative waves, worst supported entity counts, stalls, resize, pause, and low-power targets before optimizing.
Allocate a target frame budget across event, simulation, collision, rendering, presentation, and audio work using percentiles.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 8494 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to profile update and render phases against percentile frame-time, allocation, entity, collision, and responsiveness budgets without changing behavior; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. A defensible solution applies Representative Profile, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to representative frame-time and capacity evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-representative-profile — Profile warm representative waves, worst supported entity counts, stalls, resize, pause, and low-power targets before optimizing.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-performance-profiling-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-performance-profiling-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_performance_profiling_3(updates: int = 120, draws: int = 60, frame_p95_ms: float = 12.4, frame_budget_ms: float = 16.667, allocations_per_frame: int = 3, allocation_budget: int = 8, output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if updates < 1 or draws < 1 or frame_p95_ms < 0.0:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "profile-domain"
    if frame_p95_ms > frame_budget_ms:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "frame-budget"
    if allocations_per_frame > allocation_budget:
        return False, allocations_per_frame, "allocation-budget"
    if not output_equal:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "semantic-regression"
    assert frame_p95_ms <= frame_budget_ms
    return True, round(frame_p95_ms, 3), "profiled-frame"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-representative-profile-c9gtzz
# Competency: asteroids-representative-profile.
# Case 7742: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_representative_profile_c9gtzz(updates: int = 120, draws: int = 60, frame_p95_ms: float = 12.4, frame_budget_ms: float = 16.667, allocations_per_frame: int = 3, allocation_budget: int = 8, output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_c9gtzz = "c9gtzz-zztg9c-7742"
    assert evidence_variant_c9gtzz
    evidence_axes_c9gtzz = (
        "playerc9gtzz",
        "seed7742",
        "inputzztg9c",
        "framec9gtzz7742",
        "state7742c9gtzz",
        "playtestzztg9c7742",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_c9gtzz)) == 6
    if updates < 1 or draws < 1 or frame_p95_ms < 0.0:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "profile-domain"
    if frame_p95_ms > frame_budget_ms:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "frame-budget"
    if allocations_per_frame > allocation_budget:
        return False, allocations_per_frame, "allocation-budget"
    if not output_equal:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "semantic-regression"
    assert frame_p95_ms <= frame_budget_ms
    return True, round(frame_p95_ms, 3), "profiled-frame"

Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Profile warm representative waves, worst supported entity counts, stalls, resize, pause, and low-power targets before optimizing.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: The profiler identifies which change to make without a performance question.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Profile warm representative waves, worst supported entity counts, stalls, resize, pause, and low-power targets before optimizing. Artifact under review: representative frame-time and capacity evidence.

options.text: For inspect representative profile evidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Representative Profile for representative frame-time and capacity evidence.
The record names the assumption, applies Representative Profile, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Representative Profile record says representative frame-time and capacity evidence looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect representative profile evidence boundary for Representative Profile.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Profile warm representative waves, worst supported entity counts, stalls, resize, pause, and low-power targets before optimizing.
